upportive and Collaborative: Intuit is known for its friendly and collaborative work environment. Professional Growth: The company invests in its employees, providing numerous opportunities for development and advancement. Welcome and Inclusive: Employees feel welcome and valued when they join the company. Responsibility: People are given a lot of responsibility. "Fantastic Culture and Amazing People!

Working at Intuit has been a truly rewarding experience. One of the things I love most is the flexibility the the company offers. Whether it's remote work options, flexible hours, or support for work-life balance, Intuit truly understands that employees have lives outside of work-and that understanding makes a huge difference. The company also ha a strong family-oriented culture. From leadership to teammates, there'...

Remote Work Options: Intuit offers the flexibility to work remotely, allowing employees the convenience of working from anywhere. This provides a better work-life balance and can lead to increased productivity and job satisfaction. Pay Flexibility: Intuit offers competitive salaries along with performance-based bonuses and incentives. This payment structure not only rewards hard work and dedication but also motivate...

1) I love the mission of powering the prosperity of businesses and seeing that in the work I do. 2) Intuit is very focused on employee experience and they do very well at it- best benefits I have ever had, campus is really nice in San Diego with events, gym, free fresh fruit all over campus, break rooms with free snacks, great food options, and more. 3) The people are nice Great Company, Nice Employees, Embrace Chan...

* Great equity and benefits. * Organization is still building itself so there is a lot of green-shoots work and a very low technical bar. * Incredibly easy interview process and hiring screen. * The company itself is stable and not going to dramatically change. Broken culture, lots of politics and toxic leadership

Understanding leadership team, good career growth pathing, sick time, flexible work hours, constant employee appreciation and recognition, training, company discounts. employee stock option program, and 401K Amazing company

They mandated a in-house programming language that you will need to learn. Documentation for it is lacking and nobody is excited about it. Learning this language will not build your skillset as an engineer. They implemented a stack ranking policy. No pip, no support, no severence. Amazon at least gives you a runway before firing with severance so you can prepare finances, take leave, or apply elsewhere. Intuit will...
Work load can be a bit overwhelming I Love It Here
- Can't think of any at the moment Amazing company culture and a great environment to learn and grow as an engineer
Hiring process documents do not get updated from year to year when new processes are implemented. This increases workload, quality and speed of work and stress levels. Overall Good Place to Work

completely disorganized leadership only focused on reporting, writing papers. Use regular firings & layoffs to keep employees motivated. No prioritization of work, do everything all the time and change priorities weekly. No work life balance, expected to be available all hours, nights, weekends. Political, no prioritization, blame workers for problems

evidence.role: Current employee - Turbo Tax Live Advisor
evidence.text: I wish it was not seasonal Amazing Culture and work/life balance

Leadership doesn't know how to prioritize. Everything is important and there's little to no work/life balance. SLT doesn't show much care for all the humans working so hard for them. Pay is great but not worth the burnout
Tooooo much politics, your experience here depends more on if your manager is a good manager and if she/he loves you. WLB was Good, but it's turning toxic

The company culture is highly toxic and dominated by a political environment that undermines collaboration and trust. Management fosters a negative atmosphere by prioritizing personal agendas over employee well-being, often using low performance as a pretext to dismiss employees rather than addressing systemic issues or providing support for growth. This toxic leadership style creates a hostile workplace, erodes mor...
So much politics Growth is competitive Lot of Politics
Toxic Too competitive Managers are toxic and puts team members under bus Review

- The worst Data Science you could ever imagine. The team is puppet of other business stakeholders (such as Product, Sales, etc.) and the priorities change almost every other week - Data leaders lack spine. They let other stakeholders treat DS as a support team rather than thought leaders or drivers of KPIs - While other companies let their DS spend 30-50% time in deep dives or strategic analyses, it would be a good...
Rude Customers, long calls sometimes, and the fact there isn't enough time in the day Best Company to work for!

Change often and quickly, buy-in from the team is not always gathered before the change. The Reno location is not considered a growth site which has limited career movement unless you choose to relocate Best Place To Work
No opportunity to move up. Always a contractor Great place to work remotely

Cannot quantify or count the truly important things in life. Tone of voice, faith in your resolution, or constantly changing tax code. It is what it is. Let it be! Love this job! We love helping America reconcile their taxing year!
